title_en: Introduction — The Question from Hama and Ibn Taymiyyah's Methodology
title_id: Pendahuluan — Pertanyaan dari Hama dan Metodologi Ibnu Taimiyyah
content_id:
Al-Aqeedah al-Hamawiyyah al-Kubra adalah salah satu risalah terpenting yang ditulis oleh Syaikhul Islam Ibnu Taimiyyah (661–728 H / 1263–1328 M) tentang topik nama-nama dan sifat-sifat ilahi. Karya ini mengambil namanya dari kota Hama di Suriah, dari para ulama dan qadhinya yang mengajukan pertanyaan resmi kepada Ibnu Taimiyyah mengenai pendirian Islam yang benar tentang sifat-sifat Allah — khususnya sifat-sifat yang disebutkan dalam apa yang dikenal sebagai ayat-ayat mutasyabihat, yaitu ayat-ayat dan hadis-hadis yang makna zahirnya dianggap sebagian pihak sulit untuk diselaraskan dengan tanzih.

Pertanyaan itu menjangkau Ibnu Taimiyyah ketika ia berada di Mesir, dan ia menyusun fatwa ini sebagai respons tertulis yang komprehensif. Pertanyaan tersebut secara khusus menanyakan apa yang dikatakan oleh ulama-ulama Islam tentang sifat-sifat Allah — seperti istiva'-Nya di atas 'Arsy, turunnya ke langit terendah, tangan-Nya, wajah-Nya, dan sifat-sifat serupa yang disebutkan secara tegas dalam Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah yang mapan. Para penanya ingin mengetahui pendirian mana yang benar: pendekatan mereka yang menegaskan sifat-sifat ini (cara ulama Salaf), atau pendekatan mereka yang menafsirkan ulang atau meniadakannya (cara sebagian teolog kemudian).

Metodologi Ibnu Taimiyyah dalam risalah ini berpijak pada prinsip fundamental: kewajiban mengikuti Al-Qur'an, Sunnah yang autentik, dan pemahaman para pendahulu saleh (al-Salaf al-Salih) — yaitu para Sahabat, Tabi'un, dan ulama-ulama terkemuka generasi awal. Ia berargumen bahwa ketiga sumber ini bersama-sama membentuk satu-satunya panduan yang dapat diandalkan untuk memahami apa yang Allah wahyukan tentang diri-Nya, dan bahwa setiap pendirian teologis yang menyimpang dari fondasi ini adalah meragukan, bahkan menyimpang.

Aspek kunci dari pendekatannya adalah penekanannya bahwa menegaskan teks-teks sebagaimana adanya tidak mengandung antropomorfisme (tasybiih) atau menyerupakan Allah dengan ciptaan-Nya. Sebaliknya, ia mengandung penegasan terhadap Allah apa yang Dia tegaskan untuk diri-Nya, dengan cara yang sesuai dengan keagungan dan kesempurnaan-Nya, tanpa bertanya bagaimana (bila kayf) dan tanpa membandingkan-Nya dengan makhluk. Pendirian ini, ia tunjukkan, adalah sikap bulat komunitas Muslim awal.

Ibnu Taimiyyah juga menekankan bahwa ulama Salaf bukanlah orang-orang yang membiarkan teks-teks sulit tanpa dibahas karena kebodohan atau ketidakmampuan. Sebaliknya, mereka memahami perkara-perkara ini secara mendalam dan memilih jalan penegasan karena hal itu dituntut oleh teks-teks yang diwahyukan maupun akal yang sehat. Diam mereka tentang tafsir spekulatif (ta'wil) bukan kebingungan — itu adalah ketaatan yang disengaja pada apa yang Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam dan para Sahabatnya riwayatkan.

Risalah ini menjadi tonggak dalam teologi Athari, menawarkan bantahan yang terperinci berbasis teks terhadap posisi-posisi Mu'tazilah dan Asy'ariyyah tentang sifat-sifat ilahi, sekaligus membela posisi Sunni klasik yang telah menjadi landasan bersama sarjana Muslim selama berabad-abad. Ia tetap dipelajari hingga hari ini sebagai referensi utama untuk memahami pemahaman Athari tentang tauhid al-asma' wa al-sifat.
content_en:
Al-Aqeedah al-Hamawiyyah al-Kubra is one of the most important epistles written by Shaykh al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah (661–728 AH / 1263–1328 CE) on the subject of the divine names and attributes. The work takes its name from the city of Hama in Syria, from whose scholars and judges a formal question was posed to Ibn Taymiyyah regarding the correct Islamic position on Allah's attributes — particularly the attributes mentioned in what are known as the mutashabihat, or the verses and hadiths whose apparent meanings some considered difficult to reconcile with transcendence.

The question reached Ibn Taymiyyah while he was in Egypt, and he composed this fatwa as a comprehensive written response. The query specifically asked about what the scholars of Islam have said regarding the attributes of Allah — such as His settling over the Throne (al-istiwa'), His descent to the lowest heaven, His Hand, His Face, and similar attributes mentioned explicitly in the Quran and established Sunnah. The questioners wanted to know which position was correct: the approach of those who affirm these attributes (the way of the Salaf), or the approach of those who reinterpret or negate them (the way of certain later theologians).

Ibn Taymiyyah's methodology in this treatise is grounded in a fundamental principle: the obligation to follow the Quran, the authentic Sunnah, and the understanding of the righteous predecessors (al-Salaf al-Salih) — namely the Companions, the Successors (Tabi'un), and the leading scholars of the early generations. He argues that these three sources together form the only reliable guide to understanding what Allah has revealed about Himself, and that any theological position which departs from this foundation is suspect at best and deviant at worst.

A key aspect of his approach is his insistence that affirming the texts as they appear does not entail anthropomorphism (tashbih) or likening Allah to His creation. Rather, it entails affirming for Allah what He has affirmed for Himself, in a manner that befits His Majesty and perfection, without asking how (bila kayf) and without comparing Him to created things. This position, he demonstrates, was the unanimous stance of the early Muslim community.

Ibn Taymiyyah also emphasizes that the Salaf were not a people who left difficult texts unaddressed out of ignorance or incapacity. On the contrary, they understood these matters deeply and chose the path of affirmation because it is demanded by both the revealed texts and sound reason. Their silence on speculative interpretation (ta'wil) was not confusion — it was deliberate adherence to what the Prophet, peace be upon him, and his Companions had transmitted.

This treatise became a landmark in Athari theology, offering a detailed, text-based rebuttal of the Mu'tazilite and Ash'arite positions on divine attributes, while simultaneously defending the classical Sunni position that had been the common ground of Muslim scholarship for centuries. It remains studied today as a primary reference for understanding the Athari understanding of tawhid al-asma' wa al-sifat.
content_ar: Al-العقيدة al-Hamawiyyah al-Kubra is one of the most المهم epistles written by Shaykh al-الإسلام ابن تيمية (661–728 AH / 1263–1328 CE) on the subject of the divine names and attributes. The work takes its name from the city of Hama in Syria, from whose العلماء and judges a formal question was posed to ابن تيمية فيما يخص the correct Islamic position on الله's attributes — particularly the attributes mentioned in what are known as the mutashabihat, or the الآيات and hadiths whose apparent meanings some considered difficult to reconcile with transcendence. The question reached ابن تيمية بينما he was in Egypt, and he composed this الفتوى as a comprehensive written response. The query specifically asked about what the العلماء of الإسلام have said فيما يخص the attributes of الله — مثل His settling over the Throne (al-istiwa'), His descent to the lowest heaven, His Hand, His Face, and similar attributes mentioned explicitly in the القرآن الكريم and established السنة. The questioners wanted لمعرفة which position was correct: the approach of those who affirm these attributes (the الطريق of the السلف), or the approach of those who reinterpret or negate them (the الطريق of certain later theologians). ابن تيمية's methodology in this treatise is grounded in a الأساسي principle: the الالتزام للاتباع the القرآن الكريم, the authentic السنة, and the الفهم of the الصالح predecessors (al-السلف al-Salih) — أي the الصحابة, the التابعين (Tabi'un), and the leading العلماء of the early generations. He argues that these three sources together form the only reliable guide to الفهم what الله has revealed about Himself, and that any theological position which departs from this foundation is suspect at best and deviant at worst. A الرئيسي aspect of his approach is his insistence that affirming the texts as they appear does not entail anthropomorphism (tashbih) or likening الله to His creation. Rather, it entails affirming for الله what He has affirmed for Himself, in a manner that befits His Majesty and perfection, without asking how (bila kayf) and without comparing Him to created things. This position, he demonstrates, was the unanimous stance of the early مسلم الجماعة. ابن تيمية أيضاً emphasizes that the السلف were not a people who left difficult texts unaddressed out of ignorance or incapacity. On the contrary, they understood these matters deeply and chose the الطريق of affirmation لأن it is demanded by both the revealed texts and sound العقل. Their silence on speculative interpretation (ta'wil) was not confusion — it was deliberate adherence to what النبي ﷺ, ﷺ, and his الصحابة had transmitted. This treatise became a landmark in الأثري theology, offering a detailed, text-based rebuttal of the Mu'tazilite and Ash'arite positions on divine attributes, بينما simultaneously defending the الكلاسيكي Sunni position that had been the common ground of مسلم scholarsh
